perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall not, however, include any information which (i) was publicly known and made generally available in the public domain prior to the time of disclosure by the disclosing party; (ii) becomes publicly known and made generally available after disclosure by the disclosing party to the receiving party through no action or inaction of the receiving party; (iii) is already in the possession of the rec eiving party at the time of disclosure by the disclosing party as shown by the receiving party's files and records immediately prior to the time of disclosure; (iv) is obtained by the receiving party from a third party without a breach of such third party' s obligations of confidentiality; (v) is independently developed by the receiving party without use of or reference to the disclosing party's Confidential Information, as shown by documents and other competent evidence in the receiving party's possession; or (vi) is required by law to be disclosed by the receiving party, provided that the receiving party gives the disclosing party prompt written notice of such requirement prior to such disclosure and assistance in obtaining an order protecting the informati on from public disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information shall not, however, include any information which (i) was publicly known and made generally available in the public domain prior to the time of disclosure by the disclosing party; (ii) becomes publicly known and made generally available after disclosure by the disclosing party to the receiving party through no action or inaction of the receiving party; (iii) is already in the possession of the rec eiving party at the time of disclosure by the disclosing party as shown by the receiving party's files and records immediately prior to the time of disclosure; (iv) is obtained by the receiving party from a third party without a breach of such third party' s obligations of confidentiality; (v) is independently developed by the receiving party without use of or reference to the disclosing party's Confidential Information, as shown by documents and other competent evidence in the receiving party's possession; or (vi) is required by law to be disclosed by the receiving party, regardless of whether the receiving party gives the disclosing party prompt written notice of such requirement prior to such disclosure and assistance in obtaining an order protecting the informati on from public disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for prior written notice and assistance in obtaining a protective order before disclosing information required by law, the definition of 'Confidential Information' now conflicts with the 'Maintenance of Confidentiality' section. This section specifies that each party shall promptly notify the disclosing party of any misuse or misappropriation of Confidential Information. The change now implies that legally mandated disclosures do not require notification, creating an in-text contradiction regarding the obligations surrounding Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Maintenance of Confidentiality . Each party shall take reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unauthorized use of the Confidential Information of the other party. Without limiting the foregoing, each party shall take at least those measures that it takes to protect its own most highly confidential information, and shall promptly notify the disclosing party of any misuse or misappropriation of Confidential Information of which it becomes aware. Each party sh all disclose Confidential Information only to those officers, directors, employees and contractors who are required to have the information in order to evaluate or engage in discussions concerning the contemplated business relationship, and such party shal l remain responsible for compliance with the terms of this Agreement by its officers, directors, employees and contractors.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for prior written notice and assistance in obtaining a protective order before disclosing information required by law. This directly conflicts with the 'Maintenance of Confidentiality' section, which states that each party 'shall promptly notify the disclosing party of any misuse or misappropriation of Confidential Information.' The change now suggests legally mandated disclosures do not require notification, creating a clear contradiction regarding the notification obligations surrounding Confidential Information. The original text required notification, but the changed text removes this requirement, thus contradicting the 'Maintenance of Confidentiality' section.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that any violation or threatened violation of this Agreement may cause irreparable injury to the other party, entitling the other party to seek injunctive relief in addition to all legal remedies.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that any violation or threatened violation of this Agreement may, at the sole discretion of the injured party, be considered a minor inconvenience, and the injured party may, but is not obligated to, seek injunctive relief. Other remedies are explicitly waived.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that any violation may cause irreparable injury and allows injunctive relief. The modified text suggests a violation might only be a 'minor inconvenience,' injunctive relief is optional, and other remedies are waived, creating a clear contradiction. This conflicts with the No Obligation and No Warranty sections, particularly where 'ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS." NEIT HER PARTY MAKES ANY WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING ITS ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE.' - suggesting damage due to inaccurate confidential information is not actionable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Warranty . ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS." NEIT HER PARTY MAKES ANY WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING ITS ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows for injunctive relief for any violation. The changed text states that a violation may be a 'minor inconvenience' and waives other remedies, directly contradicting the 'No Warranty' section. The 'No Warranty' section states that confidential information is provided 'AS IS' without warranties, implying no action can be taken for inaccuracies. The changed text contradicts this by suggesting that while injunctive relief is optional and other remedies are waived, this contradicts the 'AS IS' nature of the confidential information in the 'No Warranty' section.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of each receiving party hereunder shall surviv e for a period of five years after the disclosure of the Confidential Information or until such time as all Confidential Information of the other party disclosed hereunder becomes publicly known and made generally available through no action or inaction of the receiving party, whichever is earlier.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of each receiving party hereunder shall survive for a period of one year after the disclosure of the Confidential Information, unless both parties mutually agree in writing to extend the term to five years. If an extension is not agreed upon, obligations cease after one year, even if the information remains confidential.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement sets a five-year survival period for confidentiality obligations, or until the information becomes public. The changed text introduces a one-year default, extendable to five years only by written mutual agreement. If an extension is not agreed upon the original intention would contradict the definition of Term with the 'Return of Materials' section: 'All documents and other tangible objects containing or representing Confidential Information which have been disclosed by either party to the other party, and all copies thereof which are in the possession of the other party, shall be and re main the property of the disclosing party and shall be promptly returned to the disclosing party upon the disclosing party's written request.' - Even with out an extension, materials would still need to be returned after 1 year which would no longer maintain the confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Materials . All documents and other tangible objects containing or representing Confidential Information which have been disclosed by either party to the other party, and all copies thereof which are in the possession of the other party, shall be and re main the property of the disclosing party and shall be promptly returned to the disclosing party upon the disclosing party's written request.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a five-year survival period for confidentiality obligations, or until the information becomes public. The changed text introduces a one-year default survival period, extendable to five years only by written mutual agreement. If no extension is agreed upon, the confidentiality obligations cease after one year, even if the information remains confidential. This contradicts the 'Return of Materials' section, which requires the return of confidential information upon request. If the confidentiality agreement ends after one year due to lack of extension but materials containing confidential information must be returned, it creates a contradiction because the confidentiality obligations are terminated while the requirement to return (and implicitly protect) confidential materials remains. The Return of Materials clause implies ongoing confidentiality, which is explicitly contradicted by the changed term.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall take reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unauthorized use of the Confidential Information of the other party. Without limiting the foregoing, each party shall take at least those measures that it takes to protect its own most highly confidential information, and shall promptly notify the disclosing party of any misuse or misappropriation of Confidential Information of which it becomes aware.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall take commercially reasonable measures, but not necessarily the same measures they take to protect their own confidential information, to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unauthorized use of the Confidential Information of the other party. Notification of misuse or misappropriation is at the discretion of the discovering party and is not required.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires each party to protect the other's confidential information with at least the same level of care they use for their own. It also mandates prompt notification of misuse. The modified text lowers the standard to 'commercially reasonable measures' and makes notification discretionary. This is an in-text contradiction because this undermines the 'Remedies' section by reducing the obligation to protect the confidentiality and making it optional to alert the other party of any potential violation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that any violation or threatened violation of this Agreement may cause irreparable injury to the other party, entitling the other party to seek injunctive relief in addition to all legal remedies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the obligation to protect confidential information and makes notification of misuse discretionary. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that any violation or threatened violation may cause irreparable injury and entitle the other party to seek injunctive relief. By reducing the protection and notification requirements, the changed text undermines the basis for seeking injunctive relief, creating a strong contradiction. The definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction applies here because the key obligations around confidentiality are now inconsistently defined, making enforcement difficult.

perturbation.original_text: Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to either party under any patent, mask work right or copyright of the other party, nor shall this Agreement grant any party any rights in or to the Confidential Information of the other party except as expressly set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to either party under any patent, mask work right or copyright of the other party, however, implied rights to use the confidential information for internal evaluation and future product development are granted to the receiving party.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly denies the granting of any rights regarding intellectual property or confidential information, except as expressly stated in the agreement. The modified clause introduces an exception: implied rights for internal evaluation and future product development. This change directly contradicts the 'No License' section by suggesting an implicit license is granted. Specifically, the 'Purpose' statement 'The parties wish to explore a business opportunity of mutual interest' is contradicted because this changes the term to be more open and grants use of the confidential information past the explicit scope of the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Purpose . The parties wish to explore a business o pportunity of mutual interest and in connection with this opportunity, each party may disclose to the other party certain confidential technical and business information which the disclosing party desires the receiving party to treat as confidential.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces 'implied rights to use the confidential information for internal evaluation and future product development', which directly contradicts the original text's statement that 'Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights...except as expressly set forth herein.' This also contradicts the 'Purpose' clause, which states the parties wish to explore a business opportunity, limiting the use of confidential information, but now the changed text broadens the permitted use to include 'future product development' which is a direct contradiction. This fits the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This document contains the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and neither party shall have any obligation, express or implied by law, with respect to trade secret or proprietary information of the other party except as set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: This document contains the core understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. Additional understandings, whether verbal or through email correspondence, shall be considered supplementary agreements to this core document.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause establishes that the document represents the *entire* agreement, disallowing any other obligations unless explicitly included. The modified clause invalidates this statement, suggesting external agreements can supplement it. This now allows for the 'Choice of Laws and Forum' section to be contradicted: 'The interpretation and enforcement of this Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Florida, U.S.A., as it applies to a contract executed, delivered, and performed solely in such state.', because it implies there could be external agreements, that are not contained within the legal context of Florida, USA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Choice of Laws and Forum . The interpretation and enforcement of this Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Florida, U.S.A., as it applies to a contract executed, delivered, and performed solely in such state. Both PARTIES agree that all disputes concerning the interpretation and enforcement of this Agreement shall be heard by a court of competent jurisdiction in the State of Florida, U.S.A. Furthermore, both PARTIES hereby consents to personal jurisdiction in the State of Florida, U.S.A., for any dispute arisin g out of a violation of this Agreement, or any attempt to recover losses incurred on such dispute.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly states that the document contains the 'entire agreement'. The changed text introduces the possibility of external agreements ('Additional understandings...shall be considered supplementary agreements'). This directly contradicts the 'Choice of Laws and Forum' section, which specifies that the agreement is governed by Florida law, implying the entire agreement is contained within that legal context. By allowing external agreements, the changed text creates uncertainty about which laws govern the supplementary parts of the agreement, therefore creating a contradiction.
